reforms.description: President Carter proposed a job tax credit designed to benefit low-income or handicapped unemployed individuals, allowing employers to claim a tax credit based on a percentage of the employee's wages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The job tax credit incentivizes hiring among employers, potentially increasing employment and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: President Carter proposed a job tax credit designed to benefit an estimated 1.9 million low-income or handicapped unemployed people.
reforms.importance_rationale: The job tax credit represents a significant policy shift aimed at improving employment opportunities for disadvantaged groups, with potential positive effects on the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: President Carter proposes a job tax credit designed to benefit an estimated 1.9 million low-income or handicapped unemployed people between 18 and 24 years of age.
